name: Jamaica
weatherSummary: Sunny and dry Dec–Apr. Hurricane risk Aug–Oct. Year-round warm at 80–90°F.
planningNotes: Montego Bay and Negril are most tourist-friendly. Ocho Rios for waterfalls. Book all-inclusive Dec–Feb 3+ months ahead.

name: Aruba
weatherSummary: The most consistent weather in the Caribbean. Outside the hurricane belt. 82°F year-round with trade wind breezes. Almost no rain.
planningNotes: Eagle Beach is less crowded than Palm Beach. Ideal destination for wind sports (kitesurfing, windsurfing). Pricier than most Caribbean islands.

name: Curaçao
weatherSummary: Outside the hurricane belt. Dry and sunny most of the year. Brief rain showers Oct–Jan. Avg 86°F.
planningNotes: Willemstad UNESCO old town is worth a full day. Better value than Aruba with similar weather protection. Great snorkeling off shore without boats.

name: Turks & Caicos
weatherSummary: Grace Bay Beach consistently rated world's best. Dec–Apr is dry season at 80–84°F. Aug–Sep hurricane season.
planningNotes: Grace Bay is the most accessible. North Caicos for off-the-beaten-path. Budget: one of the most expensive Caribbean destinations — expect $5,000+ for a week for two.

name: St. Lucia
weatherSummary: Dramatic volcanic landscape with lush rainforest. Dry season Jan–May. Avg 80°F. Humid Jun–Nov.
planningNotes: The Pitons are iconic — hike Gros Piton for full-day adventure. Rodney Bay for nightlife and sailing. Castries for shopping. Known as the honeymoon island.

name: Cayman Islands
weatherSummary: Seven Mile Beach is world-class. Dec–May is peak season. Avg 82°F year-round. Hurricane risk Aug–Oct.
planningNotes: Grand Cayman is the main island. Stingray City sandbar is a unique experience. Very safe destination. Expensive — comparable to Turks & Caicos.

name: Mykonos
weatherSummary: Hot Mediterranean summers. July–Aug is peak (and very crowded). May–Jun and Sep–Oct offer warm weather with fewer crowds. Winter is quiet and many businesses close.
planningNotes: Book accommodations 4–6 months ahead for summer. Little Venice is the scenic neighborhood. Windmills and Chora old town for photos. Party scene is world-famous.

name: Capri
weatherSummary: Mediterranean climate. May–Jun and Sep–Oct are ideal — warm, sunny, manageable crowds. July–Aug is very hot and extremely crowded with day-trippers.
planningNotes: Stay overnight to experience the island after day-trippers leave. Blue Grotto sea cave is the top attraction — book morning slots. Anacapri is quieter than Capri town.

name: Positano
weatherSummary: Cliffside Amalfi Coast town. May–Jun and Sep–Oct are ideal — warm without peak summer congestion. July–Aug is scorching and gridlocked.
planningNotes: Most hotels lack elevators — stairs everywhere. Rent a car for Amalfi Coast drive (book early). Ferry from Salerno/Sorrento is often faster than driving in summer.

name: Lake Como
weatherSummary: Alpine lake setting with mild springs and autumns. May–Jun and Sep–Oct are the sweet spot. July–Aug is hot and crowded. Winter is cold and many hotels close.
planningNotes: Base in Varenna or Bellagio (smaller and more charming than Como town). Ferry between villages is essential. Day-trippable from Milan in 1 hour by train.

name: Zakynthos (Zante)
weatherSummary: Navagio (Shipwreck Beach) is one of the world's most photographed. May–Jun and Sep–Oct offer warm weather, good visibility for sea turtles. July–Aug is very crowded.
planningNotes: Loggerhead sea turtle nesting Jun–Aug — Laganas Beach has nesting restrictions. Blue Caves best visited morning for best light. Ferries from Patras (Greece) or Brindisi (Italy).

name: Oahu, Hawaii
weatherSummary: North Shore has big wave surfing Nov–Feb. South shore (Waikiki) is calm year-round for swimming. Peak crowds Dec–Jan. Apr–Oct is ideal for most travelers.
planningNotes: Honolulu for urban experience. North Shore for surf culture and shrimp trucks. Pearl Harbor requires advance ticket booking. Road to Hana is on Maui — take a separate trip.

name: Moorea, French Polynesia
weatherSummary: May–Oct is dry season with low humidity at 77–84°F. Dec–Mar is wet season with tropical rains. Year-round warm — it's a question of rain vs. dry.
planningNotes: Just 30 minutes by ferry from Tahiti. Overwater bungalows are the experience. Swim with humpback whales Jul–Oct. Far cheaper than Bora Bora with comparable beauty.

name: Swiss Alps
weatherSummary: Two peak seasons: ski season Dec–Mar and summer hiking Jul–Aug. Spring (Apr–May) and autumn (Nov) are shoulder — many lifts closed. Summer is surprisingly warm at altitude.
planningNotes: Interlaken is a budget-friendly base. Zermatt (Matterhorn) and Grindelwald are premium. Swiss Pass covers trains, boats, and most buses — essential for multi-city visits.

name: Canadian Rockies
weatherSummary: Summer (Jun–Sep) is the main season at 60–75°F. Banff and Jasper are snow-covered Oct–May. The turquoise glacial lakes (Moraine, Louise) are most vibrant Jun–Sep.
planningNotes: Moraine Lake requires the Parks Canada shuttle or very early arrival. Book summer accommodations 6+ months ahead. Icefields Parkway is one of the world's great drives.

name: Tuscany
weatherSummary: Spring (Apr–Jun) and early autumn (Sep–Oct) are ideal — rolling hills green, temperatures 65–75°F, harvest in September. July–Aug is very hot and crowded in Florence and Siena.
planningNotes: Base in a countryside agriturismo for the authentic experience. Florence for art (book Uffizi weeks ahead). Siena for medieval atmosphere. Rent a car for the Val d'Orcia countryside.

name: Lisbon
weatherSummary: One of Western Europe's sunniest cities. Spring and autumn are best: 65–75°F with low crowds. July–Aug is very hot (90°F+) with peak tourist numbers.
planningNotes: Alfama neighborhood for Fado music and viewpoints. Day trips to Sintra (fairy-tale palaces) are 40 min by train. Portugal is one of Western Europe's most affordable destinations.

name: Canterbury
weatherSummary: Best May–Sep. Summers are mild at 65–70°F. Winters are grey and cold. A manageable city break in 1–2 days.
planningNotes: 1 hour from London by train. Canterbury Cathedral is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Combine with Dover Castle and white cliffs. UK ETA required for most non-UK/Irish visitors from 2026.

name: Zanzibar
weatherSummary: Two monsoon seasons: long rains Apr–May, short rains Nov. Jun–Oct is the dry season — clear skies, calm seas, good diving visibility. Dec–Feb is warm and increasingly popular.
planningNotes: Stone Town UNESCO old city is worth 2 full days. Nungwi (north) for beach parties; Paje (east) for kitesurfing. Combine with a Tanzania mainland safari for the ultimate trip.

name: Bali
weatherSummary: Dry season Apr–Oct is ideal with low humidity and clear skies. Wet season Nov–Mar brings afternoon downpours — still warm but can disrupt outdoor plans.
planningNotes: Ubud for culture and rice terraces. Seminyak for beach clubs. Canggu for the digital nomad scene. Avoid Kuta for anything other than cheap beer. One of the best value destinations globally.

name: Hoi An
weatherSummary: Feb–Aug is dry season with hot temperatures 77–90°F. Oct–Nov is typhoon season with flooding risk in the ancient town. The lantern-lit streets are magical year-round.
planningNotes: UNESCO Ancient Town is car-free — explore by bicycle. An Bang Beach is 5km away. Custom tailoring is world-famous here. Budget: one of the cheapest destinations globally at $40–60/day for two.

name: Oaxaca
weatherSummary: Highland plateau city at 5,000ft — warm days (75°F) and cool nights year-round. Jun–Aug is rainy season. Day of the Dead (late Oct/early Nov) is peak cultural event.
planningNotes: Day of the Dead celebrations (Oct 31–Nov 2) are extraordinary but book 6+ months ahead. Monte Albán archaeological site is 30 min away. One of Mexico's best food cities.
